_**User (2026-07-04 11:27:49Z)**_

Read `context/context.md` and `context/architecture.md` first. Then build project-local skills that encode everything this project needs to know about talking to Xero, so that neither you nor Cursor ever has to rediscover Xero facts mid-build.

## Step 1 — pull the source material

Clone shallow into `.tmp/xero-refs/` (gitignored):

- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-mcp-server
- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-agent-toolkit
- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-prompt-library
- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-command-line

Study before writing anything:
- `xero-prompt-library/*/SKILL.md` — Xero's own skill-writing style, especially the Lovable ones. Their auth guidance is Lovable/Supabase-flavoured; DO NOT copy the runtime specifics, DO copy their discipline: pre-flight checks first, exact error → real cause tables, scope minimalism, token handling rules.
- `xero-prompt-library/php/accounts-payable-symfony.txt` — the closest bootstrap prompt to our domain; mine it for AP entity/endpoint hints.
- `xero-mcp-server/src/handlers/` — the definitive list of what MCP can and cannot do.

## Step 2 — write these skills into `.claude/skills/`

Each skill: a directory with `SKILL.md`, frontmatter `name` + `description` (description states WHEN to trigger), runnable snippets in our stack (NestJS/TypeScript, xero-node SDK or plain fetch — pick one and be consistent), and a "traps" section. Terse and factual, no filler. If you cannot verify a fact from the cloned repos or official docs, mark it `UNVERIFIED — check before relying`.

### `xero-auth`
Custom Connections (client_credentials): token endpoint, scopes we need (`accounting.transactions`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.reports.read`), token caching + refresh-on-401, the fact that custom connections are single-org so no `xero-tenant-id` juggling, and the pre-flight env assertions pattern (empty `client_id` surfaces as `unauthorized_client` — fail fast with a clear message, exactly like the official Lovable skill does). Include a `verify-auth.ts` snippet that lists 1 invoice and prints the org name — this is Gate G0.

### `xero-accounting-api`
The endpoints this project touches, with request/response shapes trimmed to fields we use:
- Invoices type ACCPAY (bills): list with where/order/page, create, update status DRAFT→SUBMITTED→AUTHORISED, line items, `LineAmountTypes`.
- **Purchase Orders: NOT in the MCP server — raw API only.** Full CRUD shapes, status flow, marking billed.
- Attachments: upload onto an invoice (raw bytes PUT, content-type, filename rules, 25MB cap), list, the online-invoice visibility flag.
- History/Notes: append the agent's decision note to any document.
- Contacts: list + fuzzy-relevant fields (Name, EmailAddress).
- Reports: aged payables by contact.
Pagination (100/page), `If-Modified-Since`, `where` filter syntax and its quoting traps, validation error envelope shape (`Elements[].ValidationErrors`), and rate limits (60/min, 5,000/day per tenant; 429 handling with `Retry-After`).

### `xero-payments`
Creating a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY invoice: required fields (Invoice, Account with `EnablePaymentsToAccount` or bank account code, Date, Amount), partial vs full, common rejection causes (unauthorised invoice, wrong account class, currency mismatch). This is the judged "Payments API" moment — the snippet must work first try.

### `xero-mcp-usage`
When to use the MCP server vs raw API in THIS project (table from `architecture.md`), how to run the MCP server locally against our custom connection env vars, and the tool-name → handler mapping for the ~10 tools we actually use. Note explicitly: no PO tools, no attachment tools — those route to `xero-accounting-api`.

### `xero-demo-seed`
Seeding the demo org so the data tells a story: ~15 realistic UK suppliers, bills mostly matchable, 2 deliberate gaps (one missing receipt, one amount mismatch), matching POs raised in Xero, the Google Sheet receipt log schema (columns: date, po_ref, supplier, items, qty, signed_by, photo_link), and idempotency (seed script safe to re-run: check-by-reference before create).

## Step 3 — prove it

Run the `verify-auth.ts` snippet against the real demo org. Then delete nothing, commit `.claude/skills/` and append what you did to `context/last_update.md` per the collaboration protocol.

Hard rules while doing all of this: no business logic in skills (skills are facts + snippets, decisions live in `api/src/modules/`), no UNVERIFIED fact used in Gate-critical paths, and every snippet compiles under our tsconfig.

---

```text
1	# Encode x Xero Hackathon — CONTEXT
2	
3	Read this first. This is the why. `architecture.md` is the how. `prompt.md` bootstraps the Xero skills.
4	
5	## Event facts
6	
7	- Encode Hub x Xero, London. Build deadline Sunday July 5. 3-minute live pitch to judges, in person.
8	- Judges are Xero DevRel/product people (Regan Ashworth owns the Agentic Toolkit, Annie Terry, Sharon Ball). They know JAX, the App Store, and every incumbent. Do not pitch anything JAX or ApprovalMax already does without naming the difference first.
9	- £3,000 per track. Target track: **The Vibe Integrator** (Track 2) — "agent monitors a second app and intelligently syncs the bones of the data into Xero."
10	
11	## Judging rubric (drives every decision)
12	
13	| Weight | Criterion | Our answer |
14	|---|---|---|
15	| 50% | Xero Connection: real problem + strong use of Xero | "Can I pay this?" — the one question every AP process exists to answer. Xero is both the source of truth and the write target. |
16	| 30% | API Integration: effective use of Accounting/Payments APIs | Bills + aged payables via MCP tools, Purchase Orders via raw Accounting API (not in MCP — shows range), evidence archived via Attachments API, approved bills get real Payments created. Reads and writes, both APIs. |
17	| 20% | Architecture: reliable, production-ready design | Deterministic match engine in Postgres. LLM only at the edges (document parsing, validated JSON out, clamped). Full audit trail per decision. OpenAPI contract between apps. |
18	
19	## The product
20	
21	**Payables control agent.** For every bill that lands in Xero (or in the inbox), it assembles the three-way match — purchase order, goods receipt, invoice — from wherever the evidence already lives: Gmail/Outlook, Google Sheets, Drive. Complete match: auto-approve, create the payment in Xero, archive the evidence, log the trail. Broken match: WhatsApp the owner with exactly what's missing and buttons to act (chase supplier / confirm received / hold).
22	
23	One-liner (locked, use verbatim in the pitch):
24	> "Every business asks one question before paying a bill: did we actually order this, and did it actually arrive? We built the agent that answers it from the mess you already have, and only texts you when the answer is no."
25	
26	## Why this wins (the arguments, rehearsed)
27	
28	- **vs ApprovalMax / Zahara / Lightyear / ProcureDesk** (judges WILL ask): they sell a workflow the whole company must adopt — POs raised in their app, deliveries receipted in their app, £118–220/mo. We sell the answer to AP's only question and need read access, not behaviour change. Their architecture is the rigid if-this-then-that thing Track 2 exists to replace.
29	- **vs JAX**: JAX lives inside one Xero org and won't decide what's safe to pay (liability asymmetry — Xero captures no upside from the call and all the blame). We assemble evidence from outside Xero and take a position, with the audit trail to back it.
30	- **vs "ChatGPT could do this"**: opinions are free, accountability is the product. Deterministic matching, evidence chain, approval controls, write-back.
31	
32	## Human story (pitch opening)
33	
34	The site manager never logs into anything. The PO is an email thread. The delivery proof is a photo of a signed note on someone's phone. The invoice is a PDF in a shared inbox. Every incumbent's answer is "make all these people use our app." Ours is "leave everyone alone, we'll read what's already there."
35	
36	## Demo script (3 min, build backwards from this)
37	
38	1. Dashboard: bills in Xero, most already green (matched, auto-approved, payment created) — the product is the silence. ~30s
39	2. Live: supplier invoice email arrives (Make catches it, or direct IMAP fallback). Agent parses PDF, finds the PO in the sheet, finds NO goods receipt. Bill goes amber with the gap named. ~45s
40	3. Judge's/our phone buzzes on WhatsApp: "Bill from Thameside Timber, £4,200 — no delivery confirmation. Chase / Confirm / Hold." Tap chase. Agent emails the site contact. ~45s
41	4. Reply sent live from a phone with a delivery-note photo. Agent reads it, closes the match, bill flips green in OUR ui, then show the approved bill + payment + attached evidence INSIDE Xero. ~45s
42	5. Close on rubric mapping + the ApprovalMax line. ~15s
43	
44	## Scope cuts (already decided, do not reopen)
45	
46	- No multi-org, no accountant view. One demo org.
47	- No quotes anywhere in the demo (Xero's own Lovable example is a quote system; judges are numb to it).
48	- No user management, no settings pages. One hardcoded business.
49	- WhatsApp via Twilio sandbox, built LAST. Numbered-reply fallback if buttons misbehave.
50	- Make.com is plumbing only (OAuth + email trigger + webhook to us). Zero business logic in Make. Direct IMAP poll on the demo mailbox as the stage fallback if Make's trigger latency can't be made instant.
51	- Seed the demo Xero org tonight: ~15 bills, matching POs for most, deliberate gaps for the demo path. Data must tell a story (real supplier names, plausible amounts).
52	
53	## Sponsor visibility (free points)
54	
55	Make (workshop sponsor) used visibly as the connector layer. Lovable/Replit not used — fine. Xero MCP + raw API + Attachments + Payments named explicitly on the architecture slide.
56
```

```text
1	# Payables Control Agent — ARCHITECTURE
2	
3	Read `context.md` first. This file is the build truth.
4	
5	## Stack (locked)
6	
7	- **Frontend:** Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Built by Cursor against the OpenAPI contract. `impeccable` + `ui-ux-pro-max` skills mandatory. `frontend-design` skill prohibited.
8	- **Backend:** NestJS + PostgreSQL + TypeORM. Owns ALL Xero calls, matching, decisions, WhatsApp, audit.
9	- **LLM:** Claude API. Document parsing only (invoice PDFs, delivery-note photos, messy email bodies → strict JSON, zod-validated, clamped). Never in the match/approve decision path.
10	- **Connectors:** Make.com scenario (Gmail watch → attachment + body → POST our webhook). Direct IMAP poll on the demo mailbox as stage fallback, toggled by env flag.
11	- **WhatsApp:** Twilio sandbox. Interactive buttons if templates behave, numbered replies as the guaranteed path.
12	- **Xero:** Custom Connection (client_credentials — no OAuth redirect dance, one org, perfect for demo). MCP server tools where they exist; raw Accounting API for Purchase Orders and Attachments (not in MCP). Payments API for the approve action.
13	
14	## System shape
15	
16	```
17	  Gmail/Outlook ──▶ Make scenario ──▶ POST /webhooks/email ─┐
18	  (IMAP poll fallback, env-flagged) ─────────────────────────┤
19	                                                             ▼
20	                        ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
21	   Google Sheet         │  NestJS                                    │
22	   (goods receipts) ──▶ │  ingest → parse (Claude, zod, clamp)       │
23	                        │  → MATCH ENGINE (pure TS, deterministic)   │
24	   Xero (bills, POs) ─▶ │  → decision + audit row                    │
25	                        │  → green: approve bill, create Payment,    │
26	                        │           attach evidence (Xero APIs)      │
27	                        │  → amber: WhatsApp exception (Twilio)      │
28	                        └───────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
29	                                        │ OpenAPI (generated types)
30	                                        ▼
31	                        Next.js dashboard: bill list, match evidence
32	                        drill-down (the case file), audit trail
33	```
34	
35	## Match engine (the deterministic core — no LLM inside)
36	
37	Entities: `Bill`, `PurchaseOrder`, `GoodsReceipt`, `MatchResult`, `AuditEvent`, `ExceptionAction`.
38	
39	Match logic, in order:
40	1. **PO link:** supplier fuzzy-normalised (LLM normalises names at ingest, engine compares normalised strings) + amount within tolerance (default ±2% or £5, whichever greater) + PO date ≤ invoice date. Line-level qty×price check when lines exist on both.
41	2. **Receipt link:** goods receipt referencing the PO (sheet row or parsed delivery note) with received qty ≥ invoiced qty per line, or total-level if lines absent.
42	3. **Verdict:** `MATCHED` → auto-path. `MISSING_PO` / `MISSING_RECEIPT` / `AMOUNT_MISMATCH` / `QTY_MISMATCH` → exception path with the gap named and the evidence found so far attached.
43	
44	Every verdict writes an `AuditEvent` (input refs, rule hits, tolerances applied, actor=agent|human, timestamp). The audit trail IS the 20% architecture story — surface it in the UI.
45	
46	## Xero API usage map (the 30% story — name these in the pitch)
47	
48	| Call | Via | Purpose |
49	|---|---|---|
50	| list/get invoices (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | ingest bills already in Xero |
51	| create invoice (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | bills arriving by email that aren't in Xero yet |
52	| Purchase Orders CRUD | raw Accounting API | MCP gap — read POs, mark billed |
53	| Attachments | raw Accounting API | archive evidence PDF/photo onto the bill |
54	| approve bill (status update) | MCP tool | flip AUTHORISED on green |
55	| create Payment | MCP/Payments API | the money moment on approval |
56	| aged payables by contact | MCP tool | dashboard context strip |
57	| History/Notes endpoint | raw API | write the agent's decision note onto the bill in Xero |
58	
59	Rate limits: 60 calls/min/tenant, 5,000/day — batch reads, cache org data in Postgres, never poll Xero in a loop.
60	
61	## API contract (frontend ↔ backend)
62	
63	NestJS decorators → Swagger at `/api/docs` → `openapi.json` committed → frontend runs `openapi-typescript` to generate `web/lib/api-types.ts`. The generated file is the ONLY source of request/response types in the frontend. Contract change = regenerate + commit together. No hand-typed fetch shapes, ever.
64	
65	## Repo layout
66	
67	```
68	encode-xero-hack/
69	  context/            # this suite — both agents read before writing
70	  api/                # NestJS (Claude Code owns)
71	    src/modules/{ingest,match,xero,whatsapp,audit}/
72	    openapi.json      # committed contract
73	  web/                # Next.js (Cursor owns)
74	    lib/api-types.ts  # generated, never hand-edited
75	  seed/               # demo-org seeding scripts + sheet fixtures
76	  .claude/skills/     # Xero skills generated via context/prompt.md
77	```
78	
79	## Build order (gates, not suggestions)
80	
81	| Gate | Definition of done |
82	|---|---|
83	| G0 | Custom Connection auth proven: one script lists invoices from the demo org. Skills generated (prompt.md). Demo org seeded (~15 bills, POs in Xero, receipts sheet, 2 deliberate gaps). |
84	| G1 | Match engine passing unit tests against seeded data: greens green, ambers amber, correct gap names. **Highest-risk-first: this is the project.** |
85	| G2 | Xero write path: approve + create Payment + attach evidence + history note, verified inside Xero UI. |
86	| G3 | Email ingest live (Make webhook + IMAP fallback), Claude parse → engine → verdict end to end. |
87	| G4 | Dashboard: bill list + evidence drill-down (the case file view) + audit trail. Contract-generated types only. |
88	| G5 | WhatsApp exception loop (Twilio sandbox): buzz, tap/reply, chase email out, reply-photo closes match. LAST. |
89	| G6 | Three full live rehearsals. Pitch ×2 out loud. Hotspot configured. |
90	
91	Behind at any gate: cut from the bottom. WhatsApp buttons → numbered replies → if desperate, exception actions from the dashboard instead of WhatsApp. The match engine, Xero writes, and evidence UI are untouchable.
92	
93	## Live-demo rules
94	
95	1. Live data only. The demo mailbox, sheet, and Xero org are real; the arriving email and the delivery-note photo are sent live on stage.
96	2. Streaming/optimistic UI so latency reads as the agent thinking.
97	3. Every failure has a rehearsed fallback path that is also live (IMAP instead of Make, numbered replies instead of buttons).
98	4. Demoable at every commit. Never broken at the top of any hour.
99	
100	## Two-agent protocol
101	
102	Claude Code owns `api/`, `seed/`, skills. Cursor owns `web/`. Both read `context/last_update.md` before any write (hook-managed). Conflicts resolve in favour of the latest entry holder. The OpenAPI contract is the only interface — neither agent reaches into the other's tree.
103
```
</details></tool-use>

1	Build a Symfony application that automates accounts payable workflows

3	for API communication. Create vendor portals for invoice submission 
4	with automatic three-way matching against purchase orders and receipts.

6	extracting line items and automatically creating bills in Xero. 
7	Add approval workflows with email notifications, automatic payment 
8	scheduling based on terms, and early payment discount optimization.

10	analytics dashboards, and integration with banking APIs for payment 
11	execution. Implement comprehensive audit trails and SOX compliance reporting.
12	
13	Tech Stack: Symfony 6, PHP 8.1+, PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, Tesseract OCR
14	Key Endpoints: /Invoices (bills), /PurchaseOrders, /Payments, /Contacts
15	Authentication: OAuth 2.0 with league/oauth2-client
16	Implementation Notes: Use Symfony Messenger for async processing, implement Doctrine event listeners for audit logs

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/php/invoicing-laravel.txt`

```text
1	Create a Laravel application that generates and manages e-invoices 
2	synchronized with Xero. Use the xero-php-oauth2 SDK with Laravel's 
3	service container for dependency injection. Build a customer portal 
4	where clients can view invoices, make payments via Stripe, and 
5	download PDF invoices generated with DOMPDF. Implement automated 
6	invoice generation from project milestones tracked in the system, 
7	with line items pulled from timesheet entries. Add support for 
8	recurring invoices using Laravel's task scheduler, multi-language 
9	invoice templates, and automatic late payment reminders. Include 
10	webhook endpoints for Xero invoice updates and implement two-way 
11	sync to handle invoice edits from either system.
12	
13	Tech Stack: Laravel 9+, PHP 8.1+, MySQL, Redis, Stripe, DOMPDF
14	Key Endpoints: /Invoices, /Contacts, /Payments, /OnlineInvoices
15	Authentication: OAuth 2.0 with Laravel Socialite provider
16	Implementation Notes: Use Laravel queues for PDF generation, implement database transactions for payment processing
```
</details></tool-use>

=== index.ts ===
#!/usr/bin/env node

import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import { XeroMcpServer } from "./server/xero-mcp-server.js";
import { ToolFactory } from "./tools/tool-factory.js";

const main = async () => {
  // Create an MCP server
  const server = XeroMcpServer.GetServer();

  ToolFactory(server);

  // Start receiving messages on stdin and sending messages on stdout
  const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
  await server.connect(transport);
};

main().catch((error) => {
  console.error("Error:", error);
  process.exit(1);
});
=== tool-factory ===
import { McpServer } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js";

import { CreateTools } from "./create/index.js";
import { DeleteTools } from "./delete/index.js";
import { GetTools } from "./get/index.js";
import { ListTools } from "./list/index.js";
import { UpdateTools } from "./update/index.js";

export function ToolFactory(server: McpServer) {

  DeleteTools.map((tool) => tool()).forEach((tool) =>
    server.tool(tool.name, tool.description, tool.schema, tool.handler),
  );
  GetTools.map((tool) => tool()).forEach((tool) =>
    server.tool(tool.name, tool.description, tool.schema, tool.handler),
  );
  CreateTools.map((tool) => tool()).forEach((tool) =>
    server.tool(tool.name, tool.description, tool.schema, tool.handler),
  );
  ListTools.map((tool) => tool()).forEach((tool) =>
    server.tool(tool.name, tool.description, tool.schema, tool.handler),
  );
  UpdateTools.map((tool) => tool()).forEach((tool) =>
    server.tool(tool.name, tool.description, tool.schema, tool.handler),
  );
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Xero Agent Toolkit
2	
3	A comprehensive collection of examples demonstrating how to build AI agents that integrate with the Xero API using various agentic frameworks and the Xero MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server.
4	
5	## Overview
6	
7	This repository provides practical examples of building intelligent agents that can interact with Xero accounting data using different AI frameworks. Each example demonstrates how to leverage the [Xero MCP Server](https://github.com/xeroapi/xero-mcp-server) to provide agents with structured access to Xero's comprehensive accounting functionality.
8	
9	### What You Can Build
10	
11	The examples in this repository show how to create AI agents that can:
12	
13	- 📝 **Manage Invoices**: Create, update, and track invoices with natural language
14	- 👥 **Handle Contacts**: Create and manage customers and suppliers
15	- 💰 **Process Payments**: Record and track payments against invoices
16	- 👨‍💼 **Payroll Operations**: Manage employees, timesheets, and leave (where applicable)
17	- 💬 **Natural Language Interface**: Process conversational queries about accounting data
18	
19	## Available Examples
20	
21	### Python Implementations
22	
23	#### 🔗 [Google ADK Integration](./python/google-adk/)
24	Demonstrates integration with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK), featuring:
25	- Real-time Xero integration via MCP
26	- Natural language processing for accounting queries
27	- Interactive demo modes and batch operations
28	- Comprehensive error handling
29	
30	#### 🦜 [LangChain Integration](./python/langchain/)
31	Advanced LangChain integration showcasing:
32	- Specialized agents (Invoice Specialist, Contact Manager)
33	- Multi-step business workflows
34	- Intelligent invoice analysis and recommendations
35	- Async/await support for optimal performance
36	
37	#### 🤖 [OpenAI Agents SDK](./python/openai/)
38	OpenAI Agents SDK implementation featuring:
39	- Multiple specialized agent types
40	- Multi-agent collaboration workflows
41	- Interactive chat interfaces
42	- Comprehensive example scenarios
43	
44	### TypeScript Implementations
45	
46	#### 🔧 [OpenAI Agents SDK (TypeScript)](./typescript/openai-agents/)
47	TypeScript implementation of OpenAI Agents SDK with:
48	- Type-safe Xero operations
49	- Modern async/await patterns
50	- Interactive demo modes
51	- Comprehensive agent workflows
52	
53	#### 🦜 [LangChain (TypeScript)](./typescript/langchain/)
54	TypeScript LangChain integration featuring:
55	- Specialized agents (Invoice Specialist, Contact Manager)
56	- Multi-step business workflows and invoice analysis
57	- Interactive chat interface with real-time Xero operations
58	- Organization insights and comprehensive reporting
59	- Type-safe operations with @langchain/mcp-adapters
60	
61	## Prerequisites
62	
63	### Required for All Examples
64	- **Xero Developer Account**: Get your credentials from [Xero Developer Portal](https://developer.xero.com/)
65	  - Client ID
66	  - Client Secret
67	Make sure you are create a [Custom Connection](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/custom-connections) type of app - Web app and PKCE apps will not work with these examples
68	- **Node.js and npm**: Required for the Xero MCP Server
69	
70	### Framework-Specific Requirements
71	- **Google ADK**: Google AI Studio API key or Vertex AI setup
72	- **LangChain**: OpenAI API key
73	- **OpenAI Agents**: OpenAI API key
74	
75	## Quick Start
76	
77	1. **Clone the repository:**
78	   ```bash
79	   git clone https://github.com/xeroapi/xero-agent-toolkit.git
80	   cd xero-agent-toolkit
81	   ```
82	
83	2. **Choose your preferred framework** and navigate to its directory
84	
85	3. **Follow the specific README** in each subdirectory for detailed setup instructions
86	
87	4. **Set up your environment variables** as specified in each example
88	
89	5. **Run the examples** to see AI agents in action with Xero
90	
91	## Architecture
92	
93	All examples in this repository use the **Xero MCP Server** as the bridge between AI frameworks and the Xero API. This architecture provides:
94	
95	- **Standardized Interface**: Consistent tool definitions across all frameworks
96	- **Secure Authentication**: OAuth2 flow handled by the MCP server
97	- **Type Safety**: Structured schemas for all Xero operations
98	- **Error Handling**: Robust error management and retry logic
99	
100	```
101	┌─────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐
102	│   AI Framework  │ ←→ │  Xero MCP       │ ←→ │   Xero API      │
103	│  (LangChain,    │    │  Server         │    │                 │
104	│   OpenAI, etc.) │    │                 │    │                 │
105	└─────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘
106	```
107	
108	## Example Use Cases
109	
110	### Business Automation
111	- Automated monthly invoicing workflows
112	- Customer onboarding with contact creation
113	- Payment processing and reconciliation
114	- Financial reporting and analysis
115	
116	### Conversational Accounting
117	- "Create an invoice for ABC Corp for $1,500 consulting services"
118	- "Show me all overdue invoices"
119	- "What's my profit and loss for this quarter?"
120	- "Create a new customer named XYZ Ltd with email contact@xyz.com"
121	
122	### Multi-Agent Workflows
123	- Contact Manager → Invoice Specialist collaboration
124	- Automated end-to-end business processes
125	- Intelligent data analysis and recommendations
126	
127	## Getting Help
128	
129	- **Framework-specific issues**: Check the README in each subdirectory
130	- **Xero API questions**: Visit [Xero Developer Portal](https://developer.xero.com/)
131	- **MCP Server issues**: See [Xero MCP Server repository](https://github.com/xeroapi/xero-mcp-server)
132	- **General questions**: Open an issue in this repository
133	
134	## Contributing
135	
136	We welcome contributions! Whether you want to:
137	- Add support for new AI frameworks
138	- Improve existing examples
139	- Add new use cases or workflows
140	- Fix bugs or improve documentation
141	
142	Please feel free to open an issue or submit a pull request.
143	
144	## License
145	
146	This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.
147	
148	## Related Projects
149	
150	- [Xero MCP Server](https://github.com/xeroapi/xero-mcp-server) - The Model Context Protocol server for Xero
151	- [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) - The open standard for connecting AI systems
152	- [Xero API Documentation](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/) - Official Xero API docs
153	
154	---
155	
156	**Ready to build intelligent accounting agents?** Choose your preferred framework from the examples above and start building! 🚀
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./.tmp/xero-refs/xero-prompt-library/lovable/xero-integration-single-account/SKILL.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: xero-integration-single-account
3	description: Connect ONE Xero organisation that you own to a Lovable Cloud (TanStack Start + Lovable Cloud Postgres) app, so every visitor sees the same books through your single connection. Covers OAuth 2 (authorize → callback → refresh) with rotating refresh tokens, encrypted token storage, scope selection (granular > broad), idempotent writes, and the Lovable-specific traps around iframe preview, host mapping, and redirect URIs.
4	---
5	
6	# Single-account Xero (you connect one Xero org you own)
7	
8	This skill is for a **single-org app built in Lovable**: there is exactly one Xero
9	organisation — **yours** — and every visitor sees the same books through your one
10	connection. There are no per-user Xero accounts.
11	
12	> **First, make sure this is the right variant.** Lovable has no managed
13	> single-principal Xero connector, so you own the OAuth lifecycle either way. The
14	> deciding question is *how many Xero logins exist*:
15	>
16	> - **One — yours.** Internal dashboard over your own books, a single-merchant
17	>   store, a back-office tool, a prototype. **This skill.**
18	> - **Many — one per customer.** Each end user links *their own* Xero. Stop and
19	>   use **`xero-integration-multi-account`** instead.
20	
21	## Where this code runs in Lovable
22	
23	TanStack Start on a Cloudflare Workers-style serverless runtime, with **Lovable Cloud**
24	for Postgres, auth, secrets, and deployment. Lovable Cloud runs on Supabase under the
25	hood, so the auto-generated env variables and imports still say `supabase` (e.g.
26	`@/integrations/supabase/client.server`). App-internal server logic — including all
27	Xero OAuth and token handling — lives in **`createServerFn` server functions**;
28	external callers (Xero's browser redirect, webhooks, cron) hit **server routes** under
29	`src/routes/api/public/*`. **Do not use Supabase Edge Functions** for any of this.
30	
31	Two runtime facts shape the code below:
32	
33	- The server runtime is stateless across invocations, so the OAuth `state` value
34	  must be persisted to a table between the connect step and the callback.
35	- Use `fetch` for the OAuth lifecycle (authorize redirect, token exchange, refresh).
36	
37	**Crypto is Node `crypto`** (the Worker runtime supports it via `nodejs_compat`);
38	the three-part `iv.tag.enc` ciphertext shape below is correct on this stack.
39	
40	---
41	
42	## ⚠️ Pre-flight — do this FIRST (skipping it costs hours)
43	
44	Three checks. Each one prevents a class of bug that produces a misleading
45	upstream error from Xero. Do them **before** you wire the Connect button to a
46	human.
47	
48	### 1. Assert every secret is set and non-empty
49	
50	Empty/missing secrets surface as `unauthorized_client` from Xero (because
51	`client_id=undefined` got sent), which points away from the real cause. Fail fast
52	with a clear message inside `xeroStart` **before** building the URL:
53	
54	```ts
55	function requireEnv(name: string) {
56	  const v = process.env[name];
57	  if (!v || v.trim() === "") {
58	    throw new Error(`${name} is empty — set it in Project Secrets and retry.`);
59	  }
60	  return v;
61	}
62	```
63	
64	Call `requireEnv("XERO_CLIENT_ID")`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `XERO_SCOPES`, and
65	`TOKEN_ENC_KEY` at the top of every handler that uses them.
66	
67	### 2. Log the constructed authorize URL once
68	
69	You want a server log you can read when something is wrong, without ever
70	printing the secret value itself:
71	
72	```ts
73	console.log("[xero] authorize", {
74	  client_id_len: process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID?.length,
75	  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
76	  scope: process.env.XERO_SCOPES,
77	});
78	```
79	
80	If the next failure is `unauthorized_client` or `invalid redirect_uri`, this
81	line tells you *exactly* which value Xero rejected.
82	
83	### 3. Smoke-test `xeroStart` BEFORE clicking Connect
84	
85	Call the server function and inspect the returned URL: `client_id` must be a
86	non-empty hex string, and `redirect_uri` must **exactly** match one of the URIs
87	registered in your Xero app (including scheme, host, and path). If either is
88	wrong, fix it here. Do not proceed to the popup — Xero's errors past this point
89	are far less useful than the URL you can read right now.
90	
91	### 4. Validate `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` decodes to exactly 32 bytes
92	
93	AES-256-GCM requires a **32-byte** key. A 32-character random string is NOT 32
94	bytes once base64-decoded (it's ~24), and `createCipheriv` throws
95	`Invalid key length` from deep inside `encrypt()` — which surfaces as an
96	opaque **500 on the callback**, *after* Xero has already consumed the
97	single-use `code`. The user sees "something went wrong" with no way to retry
98	without re-consenting, and no breadcrumb in the logs points at the key.
99	
100	Make `key()` self-validating and tolerant of either shape:
101	
102	```ts
103	// src/lib/server/crypto.server.ts
104	import { createCipheriv, createDecipheriv, createHash, randomBytes } from "node:crypto";
105	function key() {
106	  const k = process.env.TOKEN_ENC_KEY;
107	  if (!k) throw new Error("TOKEN_ENC_KEY is not set");
108	  const decoded = Buffer.from(k, "base64");
109	  if (decoded.length === 32) return decoded;                         // preferred shape
110	  if (k.length >= 32) return createHash("sha256").update(k, "utf8").digest();  // tolerant shim
111	  throw new Error("TOKEN_ENC_KEY too short — must be base64(32 bytes) or ≥32 chars");
112	}
113	```
114	
115	The SHA-256 branch is a one-way compatibility shim so a mis-generated secret
116	doesn't crash the app; do not rely on it as the *intended* path. Generation
117	guidance is in §1.
118	
119	---
120	
121	
122	
123	## 1. Register your own Xero app
124	
125	In the Xero developer portal (developer.xero.com) create a **Web app** OAuth2 app
126	(confidential — it holds a secret):
127	
128	- Grab **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
129	- **Register BOTH redirect URIs**, exactly:
130	  - **Published:** `https://project--<project-id>.lovable.app/api/public/xero/callback`
131	  - **Preview:** `https://id-preview--<project-id>.lovable.app/api/public/xero/callback`
132	
133	  Tell the user to register both before first connect. The in-editor preview
134	  iframe host (`*.lovableproject.com`) and the dev-server origin (`localhost:8080`)
135	  are **NOT** valid redirect URIs — don't register them and don't send them.
136	  See the resolver in §3.
137	
138	- Decide your scopes (see **Scopes** next). You MUST include **`offline_access`**
139	  or Xero returns no refresh token. Do not ask the user what scopes to use - set
140	  the appropriate scopes for the integration to function.
141	
142	Store in Lovable Cloud secrets:
143	- `XERO_CLIENT_ID`, `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`
144	- `XERO_SCOPES` (space-separated string)
145	- `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` — must base64-decode to exactly **32 bytes**. Call
146	  `generate_secret` with **`length: 44`** (the base64 length of 32 random
147	  bytes). The default 32-char length is the common mistake — it decodes to
148	  ~24 bytes and AES-256-GCM rejects it at runtime (see Pre-flight §4).
149	
150	You do **not** need a `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` secret — the resolver in §3 derives it
151	from the request origin. `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` are
152	already available via `process.env`. The refresh token **rotates on every
153	refresh** and lives in the database (a mutable store), never in a secret.
154	
155	> Read `process.env.*` **inside** `.handler()`, not at module scope.
156	
157	## Scopes
158	
159	Scopes are **additive** — request the minimum and re-run consent to add more later.
160	You **cannot** widen scope on an existing token; you must re-consent. To get a
161	refresh token at all you must include `offline_access`.
162	
163	### User / OpenID scopes (identity)
164	
165	| Scope | Description |
166	| --- | --- |
167	| `offline_access` | required for a refresh token (offline connection) |
168	| `openid` | use the user's identity |
169	| `profile` | first name, last name, full name, Xero user id |
170	| `email` | email address |
171	
172	### Accounting API — prefer granular scopes
173	
174	Xero is replacing **broad** scopes with **granular** ones. Since **March 2026** all
175	new and existing Web/PKCE apps are assigned granular scopes; custom connections
176	since **29 April 2026**. Broad scopes keep working for apps that already used them
177	only until **September 2027**. **Always prefer the granular scopes.**
178	
179	Deprecated broad scope → the granular scopes that replace it:
180	
181	| Deprecated (broad) | New granular scopes |
182	| --- | --- |
183	| `accounting.transactions` | `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.banktransactions`, `accounting.manualjournals` |
184	| `accounting.transactions.read` | `accounting.invoices.read`, `accounting.payments.read`, `accounting.banktransactions.read`, `accounting.manualjournals.read` |
185	| `accounting.reports.read` | `accounting.reports.aged.read`, `accounting.reports.balancesheet.read`, `accounting.reports.banksummary.read`, `accounting.reports.budgetsummary.read`, `accounting.reports.executivesummary.read`, `accounting.reports.profitandloss.read`, `accounting.reports.trialbalance.read`, `accounting.reports.taxreports.read` |
186	
187	What each granular scope covers:
188	
189	| Scope | Grants |
190	| --- | --- |
191	| `accounting.invoices` | Invoices, CreditNotes, Quotes, PurchaseOrders, RepeatingInvoices, LinkedTransactions, Items |
192	| `accounting.payments` | Payments, BatchPayments, Overpayments, Prepayments |
193	| `accounting.banktransactions` | BankTransactions, BankTransfers |
194	| `accounting.manualjournals` | ManualJournals |
195	| `accounting.reports.*.read` | the matching report only |
196	
197	### Scopes that were never broad (use as-is)
198	
199	| Scope | Grants |
200	| --- | --- |
201	| `accounting.settings` / `.read` | Accounts, BrandingThemes, Currencies, Items, InvoiceReminders, Organisation, TaxRates, TrackingCategories, Users |
202	| `accounting.contacts` / `.read` | Contacts, ContactGroups |
203	| `accounting.attachments` / `.read` | attachments across most resources |
204	| `accounting.journals.read` | Journals (general ledger) |
205	| `accounting.budgets.read` | Budgets |
206	
207	**Read vs write — choose deliberately; you can't change it later without
208	re-consent.** `.read` scopes are GET-only. Write scopes (no `.read` suffix) are
209	needed for any POST/PUT. Mismatched scope → 403 at runtime and the only fix is
210	to send the user through consent again.
211	
212	Read-only dashboard:
213	```
214	openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts.read accounting.invoices.read accounting.reports.profitandloss.read
215	```
216	
217	Write / invoicing app:
218	```
219	openid profile email offline_access accounting.contacts accounting.invoices
220	```
221	
222	Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes
223	
224	## 2. Token store (one encrypted row, RLS-locked)
225	
226	```sql
227	create table public.xero_connection (
228	  tenant_id     text primary key,
229	  tenant_name   text,
230	  token_set_enc text not null,                 -- AES-256-GCM ciphertext
231	  status        text not null default 'active',-- 'active' | 'needs_reconnect'
232	  expires_at    timestamptz not null,
233	  updated_at    timestamptz not null default now()
234	);
235	grant all on public.xero_connection to service_role;
236	alter table public.xero_connection enable row level security;
237	-- INTENTIONALLY no anon/authenticated policies.
238	
239	create table public.xero_oauth_flows (
240	  state      text primary key,
241	  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
242	  expires_at timestamptz not null
243	);
244	grant all on public.xero_oauth_flows to service_role;
245	alter table public.xero_oauth_flows enable row level security;
246	```
247	
248	AES-256-GCM helpers (Node `crypto`, three-part `iv.tag.ct` base64):
249	
250	```ts
251	// src/lib/server/crypto.server.ts
252	import { createCipheriv, createDecipheriv, createHash, randomBytes } from "node:crypto";
253	function key() {
254	  const k = process.env.TOKEN_ENC_KEY;
255	  if (!k) throw new Error("TOKEN_ENC_KEY is not set");
256	  const decoded = Buffer.from(k, "base64");
257	  if (decoded.length === 32) return decoded;
258	  if (k.length >= 32) return createHash("sha256").update(k, "utf8").digest();  // tolerant shim
259	  throw new Error("TOKEN_ENC_KEY too short — must be base64(32 bytes) or ≥32 chars");
260	}
261	export function encrypt(plain: string) {
262	  const iv = randomBytes(12);
263	  const c = createCipheriv("aes-256-gcm", key(), iv);
264	  const ct = Buffer.concat([c.update(plain, "utf8"), c.final()]);
265	  const tag = c.getAuthTag();
266	  return `${iv.toString("base64")}.${tag.toString("base64")}.${ct.toString("base64")}`;
267	}
268	export function decrypt(blob: string) {
269	  const [iv, tag, ct] = blob.split(".").map((s) => Buffer.from(s, "base64"));
270	  const d = createDecipheriv("aes-256-gcm", key(), iv);
271	  d.setAuthTag(tag);
272	  return Buffer.concat([d.update(ct), d.final()]).toString("utf8");
273	}
274	```
275	
276	Shared Xero config (no secrets at module scope):
277	
278	```ts
279	// src/lib/xero-config.ts
280	export const XERO = {
281	  authorize:   "https://login.xero.com/identity/connect/authorize",
282	  token:       "https://identity.xero.com/connect/token",
283	  connections: "https://api.xero.com/connections",
284	  api:         "https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0",
285	};
286	export const basicAuth = (id: string, secret: string) =>
287	  Buffer.from(`${id}:${secret}`).toString("base64");
288	export const randomState = () =>
289	  Buffer.from(crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(16)))
290	    .toString("base64").replace(/\+/g, "-").replace(/\//g, "_").replace(/=+$/, "");
291	```
292	
293	---
294	
295	## 3. The redirect-URI resolver — read carefully
296	
297	> ### ⚠️ Three traps, all of which produce `invalid redirect_uri` or `unauthorized_client`
298	>
299	> **Trap A — `getRequest()` / `request.url` on the server returns
300	> `http://localhost:8080`.** Under the Worker runtime the inbound request you
301	> see inside a server function is on the internal dev host, not the public URL.
302	> Never derive `redirect_uri` from it.
303	>
304	> **Trap B — In the Lovable editor, `window.location.origin` is
305	> `https://<project-id>.lovableproject.com`** (the iframe host), which is NOT
306	> a registered URI. Sending it raw produces `invalid redirect_uri` every time.
307	>
308	> **Trap C — A single `XERO_REDIRECT_URI` secret can't cover both preview and
309	> published.** If you set it to the published host the preview breaks (and
310	> vice-versa). Use the resolver below instead.
311	
312	Pass the client origin into `xeroStart`, then **map** unregistered hosts to the
313	matching registered URI:
314	
315	```ts
316	// src/lib/xero-redirect.ts
317	const PROJECT_ID = "<your-project-id>";
318	const PREVIEW   = `https://id-preview--${PROJECT_ID}.lovable.app`;
319	const PUBLISHED = `https://project--${PROJECT_ID}.lovable.app`;
320	const CALLBACK  = "/api/public/xero/callback";
321	
322	export function resolveRedirectUri(clientOrigin: string | undefined): string {
323	  const origin = (clientOrigin ?? "").trim();
324	  if (!origin) return `${PREVIEW}${CALLBACK}`;
325	  let host: string;
326	  try { host = new URL(origin).hostname; } catch { return `${PREVIEW}${CALLBACK}`; }
327	  // Editor iframe host — not registered with Xero.
328	  if (host.endsWith(".lovableproject.com")) return `${PREVIEW}${CALLBACK}`;
329	  // Local dev — not registered with Xero.
330	  if (host === "localhost" || host === "127.0.0.1") return `${PREVIEW}${CALLBACK}`;
331	  // Registered hosts pass through unchanged.
332	  if (origin.startsWith(PUBLISHED)) return `${PUBLISHED}${CALLBACK}`;
333	  if (origin.startsWith(PREVIEW))   return `${PREVIEW}${CALLBACK}`;
334	  // Custom domain or anything else → default to preview, force a published
335	  // visit if needed. Add an explicit branch here if you ship a custom domain.
336	  return `${PREVIEW}${CALLBACK}`;
337	}
338	```
339	
340	Use the same resolver in **both** `xeroStart` (with the client-supplied origin)
341	and the callback route (with `new URL(request.url).origin` — which DOES work
342	correctly inside a server route because it's the actual public URL Xero hit).
343	
344	### `xeroStart` (consent kickoff)
345	
346	> ### ⚠️ Lock this behind admin auth before you connect (or accept the prototype risk)
347	>
348	> Anyone who can reach `xeroStart` can complete a connect and **overwrite your
349	> single token row with a different Xero org's tokens**, pointing your whole app
350	> at someone else's books. Production apps MUST gate `xeroStart` with
351	> `requireSupabaseAuth` + `has_role(userId, 'admin')`.
352	
353	**Prototype escape hatch (v1/internal demos only).** If you're shipping an
354	unauthenticated dashboard for v1 and explicitly accept the risk, you may leave
355	`xeroStart` ungated **only if** all of the following hold:
356	
357	- A visible, persistent warning banner on the dashboard saying "unauthenticated
358	  admin — do not share this URL".
359	- A clear plan and tracker entry to add auth before the app handles any traffic
360	  outside the team.
361	- You understand and accept these specific risks:
362	  1. Anyone with the URL can replace your stored Xero connection with theirs.
363	  2. The dashboard exposes derived revenue / order data to anyone with the URL.
364	  3. `/dashboard` cannot be indexed or shared publicly without leaking the above.
365	  4. Removing the warning banner without adding auth is a regression — flag it.
366	
367	```ts
368	// src/lib/xero-start.functions.ts
369	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start";
370	import { XERO, randomState } from "./xero-config";
371	import { resolveRedirectUri } from "./xero-redirect";
372	
373	export const xeroStart = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
374	  // .middleware([requireSupabaseAuth])  // ENABLE for production
375	  .inputValidator((d: { origin?: string } | undefined) => ({ origin: d?.origin ?? "" }))
376	  .handler(async ({ data /*, context */ }) => {
377	    const { supabaseAdmin } = await import("@/integrations/supabase/client.server");
378	
379	    // Pre-flight: fail fast with a useful message.
380	    const clientId = process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID;
381	    const scopes   = process.env.XERO_SCOPES;
382	    if (!clientId) throw new Error("XERO_CLIENT_ID is empty — set it in Project Secrets.");
383	    if (!scopes)   throw new Error("XERO_SCOPES is empty — set it in Project Secrets.");
384	
385	    const redirectUri = resolveRedirectUri(data.origin);
386	    console.log("[xero] authorize", { client_id_len: clientId.length, redirectUri, scopes });
387	
388	    const state = randomState();
389	    await supabaseAdmin.from("xero_oauth_flows").insert({
390	      state, expires_at: new Date(Date.now() + 10 * 60_000).toISOString(),
391	    });
392	
393	    const url = new URL(XERO.authorize);
394	    url.searchParams.set("response_type", "code");
395	    url.searchParams.set("client_id", clientId);
396	    url.searchParams.set("redirect_uri", redirectUri);
397	    url.searchParams.set("scope", scopes);
398	    url.searchParams.set("state", state);
399	    return { url: url.toString() };
400	  });
401	```
402	
403	### Click handler — iframe rule
404	
405	> ### ⚠️ Open consent in a NEW TAB, opened SYNCHRONOUSLY in the click handler
406	>
407	> Xero's login refuses to be framed (`frame-ancestors`), so a same-frame
408	> redirect from the Lovable preview iframe shows a **blank white page** that
409	> looks like the button is broken. Detect the iframe and open a top-level tab.
410	> The `window.open()` call MUST happen *before* any `await`, or popup blockers
411	> will eat it.
412	
413	```tsx
414	import { useServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start";
415	import { xeroStart } from "@/lib/xero-start.functions";
416	
417	const start = useServerFn(xeroStart);
418	async function onConnect() {
419	  const inIframe = window.self !== window.top;
420	  const popup = inIframe ? window.open("about:blank", "_blank") : null;  // sync!
421	  const { url } = await start({ data: { origin: window.location.origin } });
422	  if (popup) popup.location.href = url;
423	  else window.location.href = url;
424	}
425	```
426	
427	The published app at `<project>.lovable.app` is not iframed, so the same-tab
428	redirect works there — handle both.
429	
430	## 4. Callback — exchange, store your org
431	
432	Lives at `/api/public/*` to bypass Lovable's published-site auth gate; trust
433	comes from the `state` row. Use the **same** `resolveRedirectUri` helper here —
434	the exchange call's `redirect_uri` MUST match the one used to start the flow.
435	
436	```ts
437	// src/routes/api/public/xero.callback.ts
438	import { createFileRoute } from "@tanstack/react-router";
439	import { XERO, basicAuth } from "@/lib/xero-config";
440	import { resolveRedirectUri } from "@/lib/xero-redirect";
441	
442	export const Route = createFileRoute("/api/public/xero/callback")({
443	  server: {
444	    handlers: {
445	      GET: async ({ request }) => {
446	        const u = new URL(request.url);
447	        const origin = u.origin;  // safe here — this IS the public URL Xero hit
448	        const back = (q: string) =>
449	          new Response(null, { status: 302, headers: { Location: `${origin}/dashboard${q}` } });
450	        try {
451	        const { supabaseAdmin } = await import("@/integrations/supabase/client.server");
452	        const { encrypt } = await import("@/lib/server/crypto.server");
453	
454	        const redirectUri = resolveRedirectUri(origin);
455	
456	        const code = u.searchParams.get("code");
457	        const state = u.searchParams.get("state");
458	        if (!code || !state) return back("?error=missing_params");
459	
460	        const { data: flow } = await supabaseAdmin
461	          .from("xero_oauth_flows").select("*").eq("state", state).maybeSingle();
462	        if (!flow || new Date(flow.expires_at as string) < new Date())
463	          return back("?error=bad_state");
464	
465	        const tokenRes = await fetch(XERO.token, {
466	          method: "POST",
467	          headers: {
468	            Authorization: `Basic ${basicAuth(process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID!, process.env.XERO_CLIENT_SECRET!)}`,
469	            "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
470	          },
471	          body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "authorization_code", code, redirect_uri: redirectUri }),
472	        });
473	        if (!tokenRes.ok) {
474	          console.error("[xero] token exchange failed", await tokenRes.text());
475	          return back("?error=token_exchange");
476	        }
477	        const tokenSet = await tokenRes.json();
478	
479	        const conns = await fetch(XERO.connections, {
480	          headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${tokenSet.access_token}` },
481	        }).then((r) => r.json()) as Array<{ tenantId: string; tenantName: string }>;
482	
483	        const enc = encrypt(JSON.stringify(tokenSet));
484	        const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000).toISOString();
485	        for (const c of conns) {
486	          await supabaseAdmin.from("xero_connection").upsert(
487	            { tenant_id: c.tenantId, tenant_name: c.tenantName, token_set_enc: enc,
488	              status: "active", expires_at: expiresAt, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() },
489	            { onConflict: "tenant_id" },
490	          );
491	        }
492	        await supabaseAdmin.from("xero_oauth_flows").delete().eq("state", state);
493	        return back("?connected=1");
494	        } catch (e) {
495	          // Any throw past token_exchange MUST funnel into the same ?error/&detail
496	          // redirect — a bare 500 is unrecoverable here because Xero's `code` is
497	          // single-use and the user has to re-consent before you can read the next log.
498	          console.error("[xero] callback error", e);
499	          const detail = encodeURIComponent(e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e));
500	          return back(`?error=callback&detail=${detail}`);
501	        }
502	      },
503	    },
504	  },
505	});
506	```
507	
508	## 5. Use it per request (refresh + persist rotation)
509	
510	```ts
511	// src/lib/server/xero-token.server.ts
512	import { XERO, basicAuth } from "@/lib/xero-config";
513	import { encrypt, decrypt } from "./crypto.server";
514	
515	export async function getAccessToken(tenantId?: string) {
516	  const { supabaseAdmin } = await import("@/integrations/supabase/client.server");
517	
518	  const q = supabaseAdmin.from("xero_connection").select("*");
519	  const { data: conn } = tenantId
520	    ? await q.eq("tenant_id", tenantId).single()
521	    : await q.limit(1).single();
522	  if (!conn) throw new Error("Xero is not connected yet");
523	
524	  let tokenSet = JSON.parse(decrypt(conn.token_set_enc));
525	
526	  if (new Date(conn.expires_at) <= new Date(Date.now() + 60_000)) {
527	    const res = await fetch(XERO.token, {
528	      method: "POST",
529	      headers: {
530	        Authorization: `Basic ${basicAuth(process.env.XERO_CLIENT_ID!, process.env.XERO_CLIENT_SECRET!)}`,
531	        "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
532	      },
533	      body: new URLSearchParams({ grant_type: "refresh_token", refresh_token: tokenSet.refresh_token }),
534	    });
535	    if (!res.ok) {
536	      await supabaseAdmin.from("xero_connection")
537	        .update({ status: "needs_reconnect" }).eq("tenant_id", conn.tenant_id);
538	      throw new Error("Xero refresh failed — reconnect required");
539	    }
540	    tokenSet = await res.json();   // refresh_token ROTATES — must persist
541	    await supabaseAdmin.from("xero_connection").update({
542	      token_set_enc: encrypt(JSON.stringify(tokenSet)),
543	      expires_at: new Date(Date.now() + tokenSet.expires_in * 1000).toISOString(),
544	      updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
545	    }).eq("tenant_id", conn.tenant_id);
546	  }
547	  return { accessToken: tokenSet.access_token as string, tenantId: conn.tenant_id as string };
548	}
549	```
550	
551	Example write (idempotency key, `summarizeErrors=false`, `unitdp=4`):
552	
553	```ts
554	const { accessToken, tenantId } = await getAccessToken();
555	await fetch(`${XERO.api}/Invoices?unitdp=4&summarizeErrors=false`, {
556	  method: "POST",
557	  headers: {
558	    Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`,
559	    "Xero-Tenant-Id": tenantId,
560	    "Idempotency-Key": crypto.randomUUID(),
561	    "Content-Type": "application/json",
562	    Accept: "application/json",
563	  },
564	  body: JSON.stringify({ Invoices: [{ /* ... */ }] }),
565	});
566	```
567	
568	## 6. Surface connection status (reconnect UX)
569	
570	```ts
571	// src/lib/xero-status.functions.ts
572	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start";
573	
574	export const xeroStatus = createServerFn({ method: "GET" }).handler(async () => {
575	  const { supabaseAdmin } = await import("@/integrations/supabase/client.server");
576	  const { data } = await supabaseAdmin.from("xero_connection")
577	    .select("tenant_name, status").limit(1).maybeSingle();
578	  return data
579	    ? { connected: data.status === "active", status: data.status, org: data.tenant_name }
580	    : { connected: false, status: "not_connected", org: null };
581	});
582	```
583	
584	Drive a banner from it; refetch on focus. `needs_reconnect` should surface
585	immediately with a CTA that re-runs the connect flow. Refresh tokens unused for
586	60 days die silently, so on a low-traffic dashboard add a scheduled keep-alive
587	refresh, not just reactive detection.
588	
589	Also read `?error=…&detail=…` from the URL on `/dashboard` and render it in
590	the connection banner. The callback (§4) redirects every failure with those
591	params; without surfacing them, a callback crash looks identical to "not
592	connected yet" and the user has no idea anything happened.
593	
594	
595	
596	## Date + money + tax normalisation
597	
598	- **Dates.** REST returns `/Date(1518685950940+0000)/`. Normalise to ISO; send
599	  dates as `YYYY-MM-DD`.
600	
601	  ```ts
602	  export function normalizeXeroDate(v: unknown): Date | null {
603	    if (v == null) return null;
604	    if (v instanceof Date) return isNaN(v.getTime()) ? null : v;
605	    if (typeof v === "string") {
606	      const m = v.match(/\/Date\((-?\d+)([+-]\d{4})?\)\//);
607	      const d = m ? new Date(Number(m[1])) : new Date(v);
608	      return isNaN(d.getTime()) ? null : d;
609	    }
610	    return null;
611	  }
612	  ```
613	
614	- **Money.** Numbers, not formatted strings. Control rounding with `unitdp` (≤4).
615	  Postgres `numeric` comes back as strings via the Supabase client — convert before parse.
616	
617	- **Tax is your org's.** Your org's tax config is applied to invoices, so
618	  `AmountDue` / `Total` may exceed line amounts (e.g. 15% GST turns 2495 into
619	  2869.25). The `UnitAmount` you posted is still correct — don't "fix" the
620	  discrepancy. Set `LineAmountTypes` (`Exclusive`/`Inclusive`/`NoTax`) deliberately.
621	
622	## Idempotency (avoid duplicate writes)
623	
624	Two layers.
625	
626	**Layer 1 — app level:** dedup the logical action (stable order id, unique
627	constraint) so a double-submit never reaches Xero twice; upsert contacts by email.
628	
629	**Layer 2 — Xero idempotency:** `Idempotency-Key` header on mutating calls.
630	
631	Rules that bite:
632	- Only POST/PUT/PATCH honour the key; ignored on GET.
633	- Max 128 chars. A `crypto.randomUUID()` (36) is plenty.
634	- Keys expire after ~6 minutes — protects retries, not long-term dedup.
635	- Same key + different request → `400`. Persist the key and replay identically.
636	- Errors are cached too. To recover after an errored keyed request, GET to check
637	  whether the resource was created, then retry with a **new** key.
638	
639	## Other Xero gotchas
640	
641	- **`offline_access` is mandatory for refresh.** Omit it → access token dies in
642	  ~30 min with no recovery.
643	- **Token lifetimes:** access ~30 min; refresh ~60 days and **rotates on every
644	  refresh** — persist or break. Refresh dies if unused for 60 days.
645	- **Always send `Xero-Tenant-Id`** on every API call — there is no implicit org.
646	- **Contacts aren't deduplicated by email.** Look up by email/`ContactID` via
647	  `GET /Contacts` and reuse the id.
648	- **Invoice status flow:** `DRAFT` → `SUBMITTED` → `AUTHORISED`; only
649	  `AUTHORISED` are real receivables and expose `OnlineInvoiceUrl`. Use `ACCREC`
650	  (sales) vs `ACCPAY` (bills) correctly.
651	- **Rate limits:** ~60/min, 5,000/day, ~5 concurrent. On **429** read
652	  `Retry-After` and back off.
653	- **`summarizeErrors=false`** on batch creates returns per-item errors.
654	
655	### Lovable / TanStack Start gotchas
656	
657	- **Callback MUST live under `src/routes/api/public/`** — that prefix bypasses
658	  Lovable's published-site auth gate.
659	- **`xeroStart` MUST be admin-gated in production** (or accept the prototype
660	  risks in §3).
661	- **`state` MUST be persisted** — connect and callback are separate invocations.
662	- **Never import `@/integrations/supabase/client.server` at module scope** of a
663	  `*.functions.ts` or route file. Load it inside the handler.
664	- **`process.env.*` is server-only and must be read inside the handler.**
665	- **`getRequest()` / `request.url` inside a server function returns
666	  `http://localhost:8080`** under the Worker runtime — useless for `redirect_uri`.
667	- **`window.location.origin` in the editor is `*.lovableproject.com`** — also
668	  not registered. Always run it through `resolveRedirectUri`.
669	- **Service-role key never goes to the browser.**
670	
671	## Security checklist
672	
673	- Encrypt the stored token set at rest (`TOKEN_ENC_KEY` in secrets, rotating
674	  token in DB).
675	- Verify `state` on every callback against the persisted flow row; delete after
676	  use (single-use, replay-proof).
677	- `xeroStart` admin-gated, or the prototype escape hatch with banner + risks
678	  acknowledged.
679	- Callback is the only public endpoint; only acts on a valid, unexpired,
680	  single-use `state`.
681	- RLS on the token table with no user policies; no GRANT to anon/authenticated.
682	- Never expose the token or service-role key to the browser.
683	- On refresh failure, mark `needs_reconnect` and prompt re-connect.
684	- Keep all Xero access in one place (`xero-config.ts` + `xero-token.server.ts`
685	  + `xero-redirect.ts`).
686	
687	## Verify
688	
689	0. **Smoke-test before clicking Connect.** Call `xeroStart` and inspect the
690	   returned URL — `client_id` non-empty, `redirect_uri` exactly matches a URI
691	   registered in your Xero app. Fix before touching the popup.
692	0.5. **Force an encryption failure once.** Temporarily set `TOKEN_ENC_KEY` to
693	   a 16-char string and click Connect. Confirm `/dashboard` shows
694	   `?error=callback&detail=…` (with a readable message) rather than a blank
695	   500 — this proves both the callback try/catch (§4) and the dashboard
696	   banner (§6) actually wire failures back to the user. Restore the real
697	   key afterwards.
698	1. Connect your org; confirm one `xero_connection` row and a real read works.
699	2. Force a near-expiry token; confirm transparent refresh **and** rotated
700	   refresh token persisted.
701	3. Revoke the app in Xero; confirm the banner flips to `needs_reconnect`
702	   instead of looping.
703	4. Hit the callback route in a browser; confirm it isn't blocked by a login
704	   wall (proof it's under `/api/public/`).
705	5. Confirm `xeroStart` rejects non-admin callers (production gating).
706	6. If your app writes: confirm a `POST` succeeds (proof scopes carry write).
707	7. Flip a row to `needs_reconnect`; confirm banner CTA re-runs connect.
708
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Xero Prompt Library
2	
3	A comprehensive collection of prompts designed for use with Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI coding tools to develop applications and integrations with the Xero API.
4	
5	## Overview
6	
7	This repository contains carefully crafted prompts that help developers quickly bootstrap projects and build integrations with Xero's accounting API. Each prompt provides detailed specifications, requirements, and guidance for creating specific types of applications across various programming languages and frameworks.
8	
9	## How to Use
10	
11	1. **Browse the collection** - Navigate through the organized folders to find prompts that match your project needs
12	2. **Copy the prompt** - Open the `.txt` file containing the prompt you want to use
13	3. **Paste into your AI tool** - Copy the entire prompt and paste it into your preferred IDE with AI capabilities (like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or ChatGPT)
14	4. **Follow the AI's guidance** - Let the AI tool guide you through building your Xero integration
15	
16	## Repository Structure
17	
18	The prompts are organized by programming language and platform:
19	
20	### 📁 **dotnet/**
21	- `dashboard-blazor.txt` - Blazor dashboard application
22	
23	### 📁 **java/**
24	- `inventory-spring.txt` - Spring-based inventory management system
25	
26	### 📁 **javascript/**
27	- `erp-angular.txt` - Angular ERP system
28	- `pos-react-node.txt` - React/Node.js point-of-sale system
29	- `real-estate-nextjs.txt` - Next.js real estate management platform
30	
31	### 📁 **lovable/**
32	- `request-a-quote-system.txt` - Quote request system for Lovable platform
33	
34	### 📁 **miscellaneous/**
35	- `transaction-processing-rust.txt` - Rust-based transaction processing
36	- `webhook-processor-go.txt` - Go webhook processor
37	
38	### 📁 **mobile/**
39	- `expense-management-react-native.txt` - React Native expense management app
40	- `invoice-scanner-flutter.txt` - Flutter invoice scanning application
41	- `pos-kotlin.txt` - Kotlin point-of-sale mobile app
42	
43	### 📁 **php/**
44	- `accounts-payable-symfony.txt` - Symfony accounts payable system
45	- `invoicing-laravel.txt` - Laravel invoicing application
46	
47	### 📁 **python/**
48	- `ecommerce-python-django.txt` - Django e-commerce platform
49	- `ecommerce-python-flask.txt` - Flask e-commerce solution
50	- `payroll-django-celery.txt` - Django/Celery payroll system
51	- `time-tracking-vue-fast-api.txt` - Vue.js/FastAPI time tracking application
52	
53	### 📁 **replit/**
54	- `appointment-booking-system.txt` - Appointment booking system for Replit
55	
56	### 📁 **ruby/**
57	- `crm-rails.txt` - Ruby on Rails CRM system
58	
59	## What's Included in Each Prompt
60	
61	Each prompt typically includes:
62	
63	- **Project overview** and business requirements
64	- **Technical specifications** and architecture guidelines
65	- **Xero API integration** requirements and endpoints
66	- **User interface** mockups and design guidelines
67	- **Database schema** and data modeling requirements
68	- **Authentication and security** considerations
69	- **Testing and deployment** instructions
70	
71	## Getting Started with Xero API
72	
73	Before using these prompts, you'll need:
74	
75	1. **Xero Developer Account** - Sign up at [developer.xero.com](https://developer.xero.com)
76	2. **API Keys** - Create an app in the Xero Developer Portal to get your API credentials
77	3. **OAuth 2.0 Setup** - Configure OAuth for secure API access
78	4. **API Documentation** - Familiarize yourself with the [Xero API documentation](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/)
79	
80	## Supported AI Tools
81	
82	These prompts work well with:
83	
84	- **Cursor** - AI-powered code editor
85	- **GitHub Copilot** - AI pair programmer
86	- **ChatGPT** - OpenAI's conversational AI
87	- **Claude code** - Anthropic's AI coding assistant
88	- **Any LLM-powered coding assistant**
89	
90	## Tips for Best Results
91	
92	1. **Be specific** - When using a prompt, provide additional context about your specific business needs
93	2. **Iterate** - Use the AI's initial output as a starting point and refine based on your requirements
94	3. **Test thoroughly** - Always test your Xero API integrations in the sandbox environment first
95	4. **Follow best practices** - Implement proper error handling, rate limiting, and security measures
96	
97	## Contributing
98	
99	This is a curated collection of prompts. If you have suggestions for new prompts or improvements to existing ones, please feel free to contribute to the repository.
100	
101	## License
102	
103	This repository is provided as-is for educational and development purposes. Please ensure you comply with Xero's API terms of service when building integrations.
104	
105	## Support
106	
107	For Xero API-specific questions, refer to:
108	- [Xero Developer Documentation](https://developer.xero.com/documentation/)
109	- [Xero Developer Community](https://developer.xero.com/community/)
110	- [Xero API Status](https://status.developer.xero.com/)
111	
112	---
113	
114	**Happy coding! 🚀**
```
